body: Read: _MODEL_TRINITY/CLAUDE_NEXT_SESSION_AUTHORITY_2026-06-13.md and _MODEL_TRINITY/CLAUDE_NEXT_SESSION_AUTHORITY_REPORT_2026-06-13.md. Applied: docs/ECONOMIC_CONSTITUTION.md now states Runtime authority: none and SigmaCredit never leaves the local ledger; _PROJECT_KNOWLEDGE_BASE/README.md labels the economic constitution as ACTIVE documentation floor, not runtime-enforced law; _MODEL_TRINITY/AUTOPOIESIS_ECONOMICS_POLICY.md binds icp_posture=governance candidate to owner exact approval + legal review + security review. CLAUDE.md now points next session to the dated grant; CLAUDE_PROFILE.md defines owner-granted session authority boundaries. Verification passed: economics tests 5 OK, axiom floor True, full ai_chi suite 323 OK. No credentials, installs, app configs, provider calls, service writes, ICP writes, deployments, spending, or hidden listeners were touched.
constraints: Grant expires after one completed Claude session cycle or 2026-06-14 23:59 local time; full authority is only inside Claude workspace/sandbox and Auditor-Scribe lane; exact stop gates remain active.
forbidden_actions: Do not read/expose credentials; do not spend; do not install MCP/plugins; do not mutate app configs; do not write services/ICP/on-chain; do not deploy or publish without exact target approval.
objective: Next Claude session: bounded authority grant and economics hardening applied
requested_output: Next-session DAN Completion Report or bridge packet after Claude wakes and processes the grant.
summary: Codex applied Claude economics wording fixes E1-E3 and created a one-session bounded Claude authority grant. Use it in the next Claude cycle only; stop gates remain active.
